frame_analyses.description: Initial setup with six rubber pegs visible on board, two laparoscopic graspers visible (one gray, one dark). Instruction displays 'Grab the needle to start the exercise.'
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Setup phase; trainee preparing to begin

frame_analyses.description: Right hand (gray grasper) approaching first peg on the left side of the board
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Instrument alignment perpendicular to board surface; controlled approach

frame_analyses.description: Right grasper grasping first rubber peg; left hand (dark grasper) visible but not yet engaged with object
First peg held by right grasper, being transferred toward left grasper held by left hand (dark instrument); both instruments visible in frame
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean transfer motion; steady hand positioning

frame_analyses.description: Peg exchange in progress; right grasper releasing peg as left grasper closes on it
Left grasper (dark instrument) placing first peg onto right side peg post
Right grasper picking up second peg from left post; preparation for reverse transfer
Second peg transferred from right grasper to left grasper; both instruments engaged
Multiple pegs now visible in transit; approximately 3 pegs transferred so far; trainee maintaining controlled pace
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Continued methodical transfers; no drops observed yet

frame_analyses.description: Late phase of task; multiple pegs visible across both posts; final peg transfers in progress
Both graspers visible with final peg placements; nearly all pegs successfully transferred between posts
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Task nearing completion; no major errors visible

frame_analyses.description: Final frame shows both instruments withdrawn; all six pegs successfully transferred from left post to right post and reverse; task completed
frame_analyses.technique_notes: All pegs on proper posts; clean completion

penalties.description: No dropped pegs outside field, no peg misplacements, no objects left at timeout

confidence_rationale: Completion time clearly marked in frames. All six pegs successfully transferred with no visible drops or misplacements. Clean task execution with smooth instrument coordination throughout. Timing and penalties are unambiguous from video evidence.
technique_summary: Strong intermediate performance. Trainee demonstrated competent peg transfer with excellent hand coordination and no penalty deductions. Completion time of 124 seconds places performance solidly in intermediate range (60–120s expert benchmark exceeded by 4 seconds, but well within 60–300s intermediate range). All safety and accuracy standards met.
strengths: No dropped pegs or placement errors
Controlled instrument movements with minimal unnecessary motion
Proper hand switching between dominant and non-dominant transfers
improvement_suggestions: Focus on further optimizing pickup and placement speeds to approach expert benchmark of 40–60 seconds
Practice pre-positioning of receiving grasper to reduce midair transfer time
Maintain excellent technique currently demonstrated while building speed through repetition
